decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 migration vs. Explore 1 maintenance
decisions.context: ENG-4419 migrating file viewer to E2, but ongoing E1 bugs (ENG-9109 saved configs, ENG-8874 API latency). Signal suggests users still depend on E1. Need decision: freeze E1 features and accelerate E2 migration, or maintain parallel investment?

arguments.for: E2 migration aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar; concentrating resources accelerates North Star metric by improving decision velocity in modern interface
arguments.against: E1 still has production users; premature freeze risks churn before E2 reaches feature parity; latency issues affect current decision-making quality

decisions.title: Invest in local-first architecture (Sift Edge) vs. cloud optimization
decisions.context: Three signals point to local-first: ENG-8309 local data-only mode, ENG-9045 local catalog TTL, ENG-9114 SQLite WAL bug. Simultaneously, ENG-9068 evaluating AWS savings. This suggests architectural tension between edge computing and cloud efficiency.

arguments.for: Local-first enables 'Sift Edge' product; supports 'Build the Moat' through differentiated offline capability; reduces cloud costs long-term
arguments.against: Dual architecture increases complexity; local catalog bugs (ENG-9114) show immaturity; cloud optimization (ENG-9068) might deliver faster ROI

decisions.title: Resolve Python Rules performance regression blocking customer rollout
decisions.context: ENG-8158 Amplitude client causing high CPU in Python execution environment. Python Rules is key 2026 product for 'Embed Deeper' pillar, enabling customers to build custom decision logic.
decisions.blockedBy: Engineering needs profiling data to identify root cause; ENG-8158 assigned but investigation incomplete

arguments.for: Unblocking enables beta customer expansion; directly impacts 'Decisions Captured Per Customer' North Star
arguments.against: May require Amplitude SDK replacement; could delay other Canvas integrations if engineering resources diverted

decisions.title: Standardize SSO/auth infrastructure before scaling enterprise sales
decisions.context: Multiple auth tickets: ENG-8852 Keycloak registration, ENG-8157 Astrolab SSO, ENG-5178 SCIM for AWS. Pattern suggests enterprise auth is reactive, not strategic. 'Embed Deeper' requires frictionless enterprise deployment.

arguments.for: Unified auth unblocks enterprise sales velocity; reduces engineering tax of one-off integrations; supports 'Embed Deeper' by enabling seamless org-wide rollout
arguments.against: Not directly tied to North Star metric; customers requesting features over infrastructure; delays other product work

decisions.title: Define data export policy performance limits before GA
decisions.context: ENG-9083 PostgreSQL parameter limit in channel policy evaluation during exports. This is a scaling constraint for Canvas data export feature, which enables decisions by making Sift data actionable in external systems.

arguments.for: Setting limits prevents production incidents; enables confident enterprise sales; supports 'Nail the Core' by ensuring reliability
arguments.against: Limits may frustrate power users; engineering effort could optimize query instead; unclear what 'reasonable' limit is without user research

timeAudit.weekSummary: High context-switching week: 30 engineering tickets across 7 product areas signal fragmented priorities. Only 8 free hours limits strategic thinking time needed to resolve architectural decisions (local-first vs cloud, E1 vs E2).
alerts: 3 one-on-ones today reduces maker time for decision documentation
No focus blocks scheduled Wed-Fri this week
22 meetings this week 38% above sustainable load; recommend declining 3 recurring syncs
Zero time allocated to 'Build the Moat' strategy review despite Sift Edge architectural decision pending
